meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: To eliminate the chaos of unversioned code while scaling SaaS pricing, treat your pricing logic as an immutable configuration schema. By decoupling business logic from your database using version-controlled JSON schemas, you gain the ability to audit, revert, and deploy pricing tiers with absolute precision and zero downtime.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: a client asks for a pricing update, you push a hotfix, and suddenly the entire billing module collapses because there was no record of the previous state. When you lack version control, your reputation is held hostage by every deployment, turning routine SaaS updates into high-stakes gambles that drain your billable hours and your client's trust.

But the tide is turning. By leveraging AI-assisted workflows like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can transform your pricing engine into a robust, version-tracked asset. This guide strips away the complexity, giving you a repeatable architecture that ensures every pricing iteration is documented, tested, and ready to ship.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Create a centralized JSON configuration file that defines your pricing tiers, features, and limits, allowing for easy version tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a structured JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for monthly_price, storage_limit, and api_calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create pricing_schema.json --from 'JSON schema for SaaS tiers'

steps.title: Implement Versioned Logic
steps.description: Write a utility function that loads pricing based on a version tag, ensuring that your app always knows exactly which configuration it is running.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript function that retrieves the correct pricing object from pricing_schema.json based on a provided version key.

steps.title: Automate Schema Validation
steps.description: Prevent runtime errors by adding a validation layer that ensures every pricing update conforms to your established schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Zod validation schema to verify that any new pricing configuration added to our system contains the required fields.

steps.title: Integrate with Payment Gateway
steps.description: Link your version-controlled pricing logic to Stripe or LemonSqueezy to ensure your frontend and payment processor remain in sync.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a handler that maps the current pricing configuration object to a Stripe Checkout Session payload.

steps.title: Establish Deployment Hooks
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD to validate the pricing schema before any deployment, effectively creating a 'version control' workflow for your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pre-build script that runs a validation check on pricing_schema.json and exits with an error if the schema is malformed.

pro_tips: Always store your pricing configuration in a dedicated repository branch to mimic immutable infrastructure patterns.
Use a 'staging' pricing file to test new tiers before merging them into the production config.
Implement an audit log function that records which version of the pricing schema was active during a user's subscription checkout.
Decouple your currency logic from your tier limits to allow for easy localization without altering core pricing structures.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that specifically check for 'breaking changes' in your pricing JSON before every commit.
content.next_steps: After completing this implementation, audit your existing client projects to identify which modules are currently 'unversioned' and apply this JSON-schema pattern to stabilize them.
content.conversion_block: Stop manually editing pricing code and start shipping with the confidence of a lead engineer. Our community provides the templates and peer reviews you need to scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
